data.title: DeFi Development partners with Perena to leverage USD stablecoin yield for SPS growth
data.description:
• DeFi Development (DFDV) on Thursday announced a strategic partnership with Perena, a stablebank built to deliver high yield on stable assets on Solana.
  • Through this partnership, DFDV will leverage its existing stablecoin reserves by minting Perena’s USD STAR stablecoin.
  • DeFi Development (DFDV) expects to generate stablecoin-native yield, currently around 15% APY, serving as a new revenue stream.
  • Earnings from this stablecoin yield will be used to cover operational expenses, facilitate share buybacks, and acquire additional SOL.
  • This structure is designed to accelerate growth in the company’s core metric, SOL Per Share (SPS), delivering long-term value to shareholders.

data.title: Cross Country Healthcare terminates Aya Holdings merger; Aya to pay $20M fee
data.description:
• Cross Country Healthcare (CCRN) terminated its merger agreement with Aya Holdings after the parties failed to extend the deal beyond the December 3, 2025, deadline.
  • Aya Healthcare must pay Cross Country a $20M termination fee under the agreement.
  • The merger required completion of an FTC Hart-Scott-Rodino review, which was delayed by a Second Request issued in February 2025.
  • The HSR waiting period was extended due to a historic 43-day government shutdown, pushing its end date to December 30, 2025—past the merger’s termination date.
  • Cross Country sought a shortened FTC review but was unsuccessful in accelerating the process. ;
  • Aya cited prolonged regulatory uncertainty and potential FTC challenge as reasons for abandoning the merger effective December 4, 2025.
  • CCRN shares down 19.6% on Thursday.

data.title: NeuPath Health announces renewal of normal course issuer bid
data.description:
NeuPath Health (NPTH:CA) announced on Thursday a renewal of its normal course issuer bid.

During the 12-month period commencing December 4, 2025, and ending December 3, 2026, the company is authorized to purchase up to 2.53M shares. The number comes to ~4.5% of the shares outstanding as of December 3, 2025.

Purchase price will be the prevailing market price.

The company said it may not purchase more than 2% of its issued and outstanding shares during any 30-day period.

data.title: Yann LeCun says Meta won't invest in new AI startup: report
data.description:
Yann LeCun, the renowned artificial intelligence scientist who is leaving Meta Platforms (META) to start a new AI company, said the tech giant will not invest in his startup, Bloomberg reported.

The unnamed company may be based outside of Silicon Valley, given the Valley's focus elsewhere, he added. “Silicon Valley is completely hypnotized by generative models,” LeCun said at the AI-Pulse event in Paris on Thursday, according to the news outlet. “So you have to do this kind of work outside of the Valley, in Paris.”

“Our best AI systems can pass the bar exam, write code,” LeCun continued, expressing his displeasure about the focus on language models. “But we still don’t have a robot that can do what a five-year-old can. We are missing something big.”

Last month, LeCun, who is Meta's chief AI scientist and the founding director of the FAIR lab, said he would leave Meta at the end of the year. He also said Meta would partner with the AI startup and give it access to its innovations.

In a public post on LinkedIn announcing his departure, LeCun, who is sometimes referred to as “the godfather of AI,” said his new company will go beyond what's been done already. 

“The goal of the startup is to bring about the next big revolution in AI: systems that understand the physical world, have persistent memory, can reason, and can plan complex action sequences,” LeCun wrote.

LeCun added that he would have more to say about the new company in the new year.

data.title: Riley Exploration Permian to sell New Mexico midstream project in $111M deal
data.description:
Riley Exploration Permian (REPX) said Thursday it agreed to sell its Dovetail Midstream subsidiary to Targa Northern Delaware LLC for $111 million in cash, with the right to earn up to an additional $60 million contingent on achieving certain volume-based performance thresholds over a five-year period.

Dovetail's assets primarily consist of natural gas gathering infrastructure serving production in Eddy County, New Mexico.

"By removing future capital spending obligations associated with expanding the midstream system, this transaction allows us to focus our expertise and capital on the development of our upstream assets including our recent acquisition of Silverback Exploration II LLC and its subsidiaries," Riley Permian (REPX) Chairman and CEO Bobby Riley said.

data.title: Tradeweb Markets reports November trading volume of $56.8 trillion
data.description:
• Tradeweb Markets (TW) has reported total trading volume of $56.8 trillion for the month of November, up 16.4% Y/Y but down 13.1% from $65.4 trillion in October.
  • Average daily volume ((ADV)) for the month was $2.9 trillion, an increase of 22.3% year-over-year and 3.6% from $2.8 trillion in October.
